mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or positive payroll months fail to outperform negative payroll months by at least 2 percentage points annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, restrictive monetary policy elevates the discount rate on future earnings and raises debt-service costs for corporations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent, or Rank IC is positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated CPIAUCSL above 310 and FEDFUNDS above 4.0 percent create a stagflationary discount-rate shock that compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: high inflation with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of joint-high-regime months, or the interaction term fails to add incremental predictive power above single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate greater than 0.2 percentage points over three months signals weakening labor income and recession risk, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Rising unemployment episodes in late-cycle expansions coincide with positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the signal Rank IC is above zero over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through earnings surprises.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes are followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations, or the Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand and margin compression for consumption-dependent sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Declining RSAFS episodes are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the signal Rank IC is above zero over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of federal funds rates above 4.0 percent and falling housing starts creates a credit-channel drag on construction and household wealth, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and HOUST falls > 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: Joint high-rate and falling-housing periods produce positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the interaction term Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A negative three-month change in the consumer price index signals disinflation that supports real earnings growth and reduces required equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative CPIAUCSL changes are followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered months, or the signal Rank IC is negative over a 2-year rolling window.
